title: Move CUA scrub to direct JoystickControl right-stick axes
summary: Operator retest disqualified 0.3.7: its direct right-hand action-axis read did not produce working scrub, while 0.3.6 still showed the older symptom where image scrub only responded while left-hand motion kept VaM's broader navigation state active. That means the real problem is not just a generic right-hand read missing from the code path. The cleaner fix is to use the older direct joystick seam that already worked elsewhere in the product family: read `JoystickControl.Axis.RightStickX` and `RightStickY` directly, and only fall back to VaM's broader free-navigation wrapper when those axes are unavailable.
reasoning: The operator repro is much sharper now. `0.3.7` proved that asking the underlying action for a right-hand axis directly was not sufficient in this lane, because scrub stopped working entirely instead of only partially. Meanwhile `0.3.6` still responded when left-hand movement was present, which points back to the combined navigation wrapper rather than to scrub math or focus logic. Historical runtime seams in the older player stack used `JoystickControl` stick axes directly and explicitly warned that two-controller arbitration matters in VR. This release aligns the current CUA scrub seam with that older solved input law.

changes.summary: The CUA input module now reads `JoystickControl.Axis.RightStickX` and `JoystickControl.Axis.RightStickY` directly before touching the broader free-navigation wrapper.
changes.reasoning: That keeps the right-stick lane tied to the actual right controller instead of requiring unrelated left-hand movement to keep combined navigation state alive.

changes.summary: The older `GetFreeNavigateVector` path remains only as a fallback when the direct joystick axes are unavailable.
changes.reasoning: This preserves compatibility without letting the broader wrapper remain the primary runtime seam.

changes.summary: This release explicitly treats 0.3.7's direct action-axis experiment as unsuccessful in the current CUA lane.
changes.reasoning: The operator retest matters more than theoretical cleanliness, so the release ladder should record that 0.3.7 did not close the bug.

verification: Stamp version 0.3.8 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build and deploy the versioned fa_cua_player.0.3.8.dll and fa_player_asset.0.3.8.assetbundle through the existing player foundation script.
Run foundation release validation for 0.3.8 and keep the generated receipt beside the versioned release shelf.
Compile the no-input seam to confirm the release remains isolated to the interactive CUA path.
In VR, scrub images and video with the right controller while the left controller stays still or is set down, and confirm the right stick still responds.
knownIssues: This release only changes the joystick read seam and does not yet solve the remaining scrub freeze or audio-desync issues on the video path.
The current subscene-save error is still unverified from logs, so this release does not claim to fix any scene serialization problem yet.
Spatial audio remains a separate follow-up seam because the current player runtime still initializes the underlying audio source as 2D.
